platform.name: Django
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: Django is an open-source framework with no vendor platform account, tenant, or dashboard signup.
The developer has a computer, shell, browser, and permission to install Python packages and create local files.
No Django project, polls app, or running development server exists yet.

developer_goal: Install Django 6.0, create the official tutorial project and polls app, and verify the first polls view in a local browser.

first_success.outcome: A locally running Django development server routes /polls/ to the tutorial index view.
first_success.observable_signal: Opening http://localhost:8000/polls/ displays: Hello, world. You're at the polls index.

route_selection.surface: Official Django documentation: Quick install then Writing your first Django app, part 1
route_selection.rule: Use the vendor's documented first-app tutorial after minimal install; no self-service account surface exists.
route_selection.selected: Quick install + Writing your first Django app, part 1

candidates.name: Quick install + tutorial part 1
candidates.reason: Official docs place quick install immediately before the first-app tutorial; part 1 ends at the polls view browser check.

candidates.name: Quick install only
candidates.reason: Install alone does not create or run an application and directs the reader to the tutorial.

candidates.name: Django at a glance overview
candidates.reason: Architectural overview rather than the named first-app path.

required_path.action: Install a compatible Python release if needed, then install an official Django release into the Python environment.
required_path.observable_result: import django; print(django.get_version()) reports a Django 6.0-compatible version.

required_path.action: Run django-admin startproject mysite djangotutorial in the intended parent directory.
required_path.observable_result: A djangotutorial directory containing manage.py and the mysite package is created.

required_path.action: From djangotutorial, run python manage.py runserver and open http://127.0.0.1:8000/ to confirm the project scaffold.
required_path.observable_result: The development server starts and the browser shows the Django rocket landing page.
A polls application package is created.

required_path.action: Add the documented polls index view, polls/urls.py, and include polls.urls under polls/ in mysite/urls.py.
required_path.observable_result: The project routes /polls/ to the index view.
The browser displays Hello, world. You're at the polls index.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.12+ for Django 6.0, or another Python version compatible with the selected Django release.
Shell access and permission to install Python packages and write local project files.

platform_outcomes.description: django-admin and startapp scaffold project and app packages.
runserver serves the development site and returns the polls index response.

excluded.item: Reading Django documentation beyond following the install and part 1 commands
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a counted product action.

excluded.item: Tutorial parts 2 and later, including models, admin, and voting
excluded.reason: Part 1 ends after the polls index browser verification and directs the reader onward.
Quick install states this is unnecessary for SQLite during the introduction.

sources.title: Writing your first Django app, part 1

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for Django, which has no vendor platform account?
uncertainties.impact: The schema forces starting_state.boundary to account creation, but the real first-success path begins at local install and project scaffolding. Counts cannot be published as verified under the account-creation contract.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy decision that either (a) treats open-source no-account frameworks as an explicit exception with a different starting boundary, or (b) defines a synthetic account-equivalent start that reviewers accept.
